title: Million support innovative quantum technology projects
why:
- The Australian Government will provide $12.7 million in new funding to support eight quantum technology projects through the Critical Technologies Challenge Program.
- The funded projects include developments such as Australia’s first real-time mine-site sensor for measuring rare-earth elements, a hybrid quantum–classical optimisation system to enhance energy efficiency in data centre cooling, and quantum optical imaging for rapid diabetes assessment.
- The program aims to test and demonstrate market-led quantum solutions addressing national challenges, providing up to $5 million for proof-of-concept projects that have advanced beyond the feasibility stage.
- These initiatives are part of the National Quantum Strategy to support a vibrant and resilient quantum innovation ecosystem.
